title: Dryopteris robur
taxonomy: Dryopteris robur is classified as a species of fern within the genus Dryopteris, which is a part of the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta, a group of vascular plants that produce spores. The genus Dryopteris is a large and diverse group of ferns, commonly known as the wood ferns. These ferns are typically found in woodlands and shaded areas, often growing on the forest floor. Dryopteris robur is a specific species within this group, with its own unique characteristics and distribution.

intro: Dryopteris robur, also known as the oak fern or robust buckler fern, is a species of fern native to Europe and western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris robur include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
